reason: 1 GB = 1024 MB in the binary-prefix convention used by these exams.
4th generation = VLSI microprocessors; ULSI belongs to the 5th generation, transistors to 2nd, ICs to 3rd.
Printer is an output device; keyboard, mouse and joystick are inputs.
CD-RW speed ratings are quoted as write x rewrite x read (highest is always read).
RAM is volatile; ROM/BIOS retain contents without power.
Malware is an umbrella term covering spyware, worms and viruses.
Backup is the only option that actually preserves data against loss; scanning/defrag/cleanup do not.
A gateway performs protocol translation between dissimilar networks; bridges/repeaters operate on the same protocol.
PROLOG (logic programming) is the classic AI language among these; FORTRAN/C/COBOL are general-purpose. Source key 'C' looks like a mis-keyed letter.
LibreOffice is open source; Photoshop, Word and Skype are proprietary.
JPEG = Joint Photographic Experts Group.
URL = Uniform Resource Locator.
SD = Secure Digital.
1 byte = 8 bits.
The standard (Windows/enhanced) PC keyboard layout has 104 keys.
HTTP = HyperText Transfer Protocol.
3.5-inch HD floppy formatted capacity is 1.44 MB.
A character (ASCII/extended) occupies one byte = 8 bits.
Application software performs specific user tasks; system software (incl. Windows) manages the machine.

The 'traffic cop' analogy is standardly applied to the router, which directs/forwards packets between networks; a switch merely forwards frames within a LAN. Ambiguous wording, but router is the conventional answer.
